talk_metadata.session_title: Patient Advocate Poster Symposium - Advancing Cancer Research Through Patient and Community Partnerships and Engagement
talk_metadata.talk_title: Cure MEC: Building a Rare Cancer Research Ecosystem
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Access to specimens remains one of the biggest barriers to progress."
Speaker said: "We need more biospecimens and associated clinical data, including fresh tissue, cryopreserved and frozen tissue, FFPE samples, and sequencing data."

key_data_gaps: No data on actual therapeutic outcomes
No quantitative metrics on biobank usage

crispro_opportunity.description: Cure MEC lacks a systematic pipeline to link collected biospecimens with molecular profiling for patient-matched therapeutic hypotheses.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We need more biospecimens and associated clinical data, including fresh tissue, cryopreserved and frozen tissue, FFPE samples, and sequencing data."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate specimen metadata with genomic analysis to generate actionable biomarker stratification for ultra‑rare MEC patients.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: search for MEC biobank initiatives

unresolved_questions: How will the biobank ensure sustainable funding and long‑term access for researchers?

talk_metadata.session_title: Patient Advocate Poster Symposium - Advancing Cancer Research Through Patient and Community Partnerships and Engagement
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We have an upper 90% range of 'always' and greater than 50% for questions about whether advocates feel heard, respected, valued."
Speaker said: "Advocates do not have access to OneDrive, period, end of story."

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that advocates lack access to OneDrive, indicating a bottleneck in information sharing infrastructure.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on ad‑hoc anonymous website may limit scalability and data security.

key_data_gaps: No outcome data linking advocacy activities to clinical trial enrollment rates

crispro_opportunity.description: Integrate real‑time advocate feedback into protocol design to reduce patient burden (e.g., visit frequency, blood draws).
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We ask questions like, how many times patients have to go to the clinic, how many times they have to go for blood draws."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model patient burden metrics and suggest protocol modifications that optimize enrollment while maintaining scientific rigor.
external_validation_needed: Review of protocol amendment histories at Lineberger
Benchmarking against patient‑centric trial designs in literature

unresolved_questions: What quantitative impact does LEAP have on trial accrual and retention?

talk_metadata.session_title: Patient Advocate Poster Symposium - Advancing Cancer Research Through Patient and Community Partnerships and Engagement
talk_metadata.talk_title: Project Cure CRC: Adaptive Trial Platform and Data Infrastructure
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Our goal is to raise the five‑year survival rate for metastatic colorectal cancer from 13% to 35% in five years."
Speaker said: "We have about 12,000 volunteers, but only a subset are involved in the adaptive trial platform."

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Project Cure CRC Adaptive Trial (planned launch)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Stage‑specific colorectal cancer biology (neoadjuvant, peri‑op, MRD, refractory)
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Broad enrollment without mandatory biomarker stratification for each disease window
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Standardized ctDNA or molecular signature required for MRD window enrollment
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial ctDNA monitoring planned for MRD arm
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Unclear power calculations for each disease window given heterogeneous patient numbers

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker noted reliance on patient council governance, which may slow decision‑making compared to industry‑run platforms.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Heavy dependence on external funding to launch; timeline uncertain.

key_data_gaps: No published efficacy data for the adaptive platform yet

crispro_opportunity.description: Need for integrated ctDNA and molecular profiling to define eligibility for each adaptive trial window.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Patients helped shape which window mattered most" but did not detail biomarker requirements.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can provide a computational pipeline to match patient molecular profiles to the appropriate trial window in real time.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: check enrollment criteria for Project Cure CRC adaptive trial
PubMed: ctDNA utility in MRD colorectal cancer

crispro_opportunity.description: Demonstrate that a data‑driven, patient‑governed platform can achieve faster accrual than traditional sponsor‑run trials.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We have about 12,000 volunteers, but only a subset are involved in the adaptive trial platform."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model projected accrual curves under different governance structures to support funding pitches.
external_validation_needed: Benchmark accrual rates from similar adaptive platforms (e.g., I-SPY2)

unresolved_questions: What specific biomarker panels will be required for each disease window?

talk_metadata.session_title: Patient Advocate Poster Symposium - Advancing Cancer Research Through Patient and Community Partnerships and Engagement
talk_metadata.talk_title: Bicoastal Community Outreach Core: Reducing Cancer Disparities
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "There is a lack of trusted information and a real distrust for science in many communities."
Speaker said: "We pay community scientists $1,000 for participation to ensure equitable compensation."

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that many programs still lack culturally and linguistically appropriate resources, indicating a gap that competitors could fill.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on grant funding for translator services may limit long‑term sustainability.

key_data_gaps: No quantitative impact data linking outreach activities to screening or early detection rates

crispro_opportunity.description: Create a culturally tailored risk‑assessment tool that integrates social determinants with biomarker data for underserved populations.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We have a lack of culturally and in‑language information and lack of resources to navigate the healthcare system."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can develop predictive models that incorporate ethnicity, language, and socioeconomic variables alongside molecular risk scores.
external_validation_needed: PubMed: social determinants of cancer risk models

unresolved_questions: How will the program sustain paid community scientist roles without continuous grant support?

assets_to_track: Norris Cancer Center community outreach core

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Cancer Grand Challenges / Matchmakers Team (sponsored by Pfizer)
talk_metadata.session_title: Patient Advocate Poster Symposium - Advancing Cancer Research Through Patient and Community Partnerships and Engagement
talk_metadata.talk_title: Increasing Patient Advocate Involvement in Pre‑clinical Research
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We created a simple low‑burden survey to measure trainees' experiences with the advocates before and after joining Matchmakers."
Speaker said: "Advocates bring hope to researchers, and researchers bring data back to advocates – a bidirectional learning loop."

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Current measurement relies on low‑response surveys; lack of robust analytics could be exploited by competitors offering more systematic impact tracking.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Limited evidence that advocate involvement accelerates pre‑clinical milestones.

key_data_gaps: No longitudinal data linking advocate involvement to downstream grant success or publication impact

crispro_opportunity.description: Provide a quantitative analytics platform to track advocate impact on pre‑clinical project milestones, addressing the current low‑response survey limitation.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We created a simple low‑burden survey... but response rate was about half of trainees."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate survey data with project timelines, citation metrics, and funding outcomes to generate impact dashboards.
external_validation_needed: Internal project management data from Matchmakers team
Benchmarking against other advocacy‑science integration programs

crispro_opportunity.description: Partner with Matchmakers to embed computational T‑cell receptor design tools alongside advocate‑driven patient‑centric endpoints.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Team Matchmakers is working to make T‑cell receptor‑based therapies more predictable, programmable, and widely accessible."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s spine can prioritize TCR candidates based on patient‑derived epitope data supplied by advocates.

cited_competitors.name: Pfizer
cited_competitors.context: Sponsor of the session and supporter of the Matchmakers team

unresolved_questions: How can impact measurement be scaled beyond pilot surveys to institution‑wide metrics?
